commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=be80463c6c5f2f36b141e4a989423e10041ba54f)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a built-in `/usage full` footer template selected by `messages.usageTemplate: "default"`, plus a sentinel loader branch and focused test.

PR surface: Source +88, Tests +6. Total +94 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection is enough to show current main treats string values as paths and the PR changes the `default` string into a built-in selector.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs maintainer judgment on the config shape plus contributor real-behavior proof, so it is not a safe automated repair candidate.

target.title: feat(usage): built-in "default" /usage full footer template (hidden opt-in)
target.body:
## What
Add a good-looking built-in `/usage full` footer, opt-in via the **sentinel** `messages.usageTemplate: "default"`. No new config field; a path or inline template still overrides; unset keeps the existing built-in line.

It is intentionally **undocumented in the config schema/help/labels** for now — a quiet, opt-in default rather than a surfaced setting.

```jsonc
"messages": { "usageTemplate": "default" }   // built-in footer
"messages": { "usageTemplate": "~/footer.json" } // user override (unchanged)
```

## How
- `src/auto-reply/usage-bar/default-template.ts` — the default lives in **source** (not a shipped JSON) so it stays in lockstep with the renderer. It is the same `openclaw.usageBar.v1` DSL a user template uses; the DSL + the contract paths it reads are documented in-source.
- `template.ts` — `loadUsageBarTemplate` resolves the `"default"` sentinel to the built-in template, before the path/inline branches.
- `template.test.ts` — sentinel resolves to a usable template.

`when` guards wrap every optional segment, so an absent field drops the whole piece (no dangling separators or empty glyphs).

**It keeps the `📚` context-window bar; it does not render limit/reset windows.** The merged `PluginHookReplyUsageState` carries no limits data (removed for credential-safety — an absent auth signal could surface another account's subscription windows), so the footer has no limits source. Limit bars are left to user templates / a plugin.

## Real behavior proof

**Tested against the real engine** (`renderUsageBar` + `buildUsageContract` from main) with a representative per-turn `usageState`, both surfaces + a minimal-state degrade:

```
[discord]
-# -
-# openai🩺 gpt5.5📌 🌗 🐌 | 📚 [⣿⣿⣿⣿⣧]272k ↕️ 10k/21 🗄 47% 💰0.0377

[telegram]   (falls back to output.default)
gpt5.5 📌 🌗 🐌 | 📚 [⣿⣿⣿⣿⣧]272k

[discord, minimal state — no last usage, no cost, no context]
-# anthropic🤖 sonnet46 ⚡️
```

`📌` = pinned/override model, `🔄` = fallback, reasoning as a moon glyph, `⚡/🐌` = fast/slow, then context bar, last-call io, cache-hit, turn cost. Absent fields drop cleanly.

Tests: `usage-bar` suite green — **29 passed (3 files)**, including the sentinel test.

## Why
The renderer shipped in #89835, but customizing the footer means authoring the whole template. This gives a pretty footer for free with one config value, at zero cost when unused (the sentinel short-circuits before any filesystem work).
target.author: marvinthebored
